column1: How a single workout is assembled. Look up the day_type from the split's day_structure, fill slots in order. Filter tokens are pipe separated: 'Squat|Lunge' means the pattern is Squat OR Lunge. 'Compound>Isolation' means compound preferred, isolation allowed. FOCAL_1 and FOCAL_2 resolve to the user's declared focal points (eligible focal points: Chest, Back, Shoulders, Quads, Hamstrings & Glutes, Biceps, Triceps, Core, Calves. Full Body & Conditioning is not a focal point). Selection within a slot: filter by required_equipment_tags against the user's equipment, skill and exclusions, then weight by Primary Goal Fit, then focal points, then prefer already logged exercises. Required slots must fill, walking Substitutions if needed. Optional slots drop first on short sessions. Sets and reps per slot come from Prescription Logic. Compounds never sit after isolation work for the same muscle. Session budget: 4 to 6 exercises and 12 to 22 working sets at 45 to 60 minutes, 3 to 4 exercises and 9 to 14 sets at 30 minutes. Slot counts are a base, not a cap: Weekly Volume reconciliation may add or trim slots.

column9: The biggest lift goes first, freshest.
Every push is matched by a pull.
Filled when session time allows, rotated across the week.
Core finishes every full body day.
Squat and hinge in every lower session.
A second chest angle or a fly.
Compound preferred, isolation if the focal muscle has no compound (Core, Calves).
A second angle on the same target.
Second focal point if declared, otherwise more of the first.

column7: Any, Prescription Class = Interval or Power
column9: The main conditioning piece: erg, bike, intervals or swings, prescribed off the Interval or Power grid row for the user's goal.
A second modality when session length allows, different to block 1.
Farmer or suitcase carry as a finisher.
